producer_note: Founded 2003 by German entrepreneur Georg Weber near Capalbio in southern Tuscany's Maremma. 60 ha estate planted with Cabernet, Merlot, Syrah, Chardonnay and Vermentino under Toscana IGT.

Established 1996 in Oakville, Napa Valley by Bill Harlan of Harlan Estate. Five hillside Cabernet Sauvignon vineyards (Melbury, Vecina, St. Eden, Pluribus, Quella) showcase Napa terroir on a micro level.
Founded 1849 in Epernay by Francois-Edouard Pol-Roger. Family-owned house with 92 ha across Champagne; Royal Warrant from Queen Victoria 1877; Cuvee Sir Winston Churchill launched 1975.
Negociant founded 1868 by Francois Leroy in Auxey-Duresses; sister to Domaine Leroy run by Lalou Bize-Leroy. Sources across Cote de Nuits and Cote de Beaune at village to grand cru level.
Founded 1878 in Atripalda, Avellino. 11 generations of Campanian winemaking; estates in Irpinia covering Taurasi, Greco di Tufo and Fiano di Avellino DOCGs. Pioneer of native varieties.
Founded 1994 by David Powell, named for a forest in Scotland. Champions old-vine Barossa Shiraz, Grenache, Mataro; flagship RunRig from 120-170 year old Shiraz vines.
G.H. Mumm founded 1827 in Reims, owned by Pernod Ricard. Pinot Noir-dominant (78%) with Grand Cru holdings in Montagne de Reims, Cote des Blancs (Cramant, Avize) and Vallee de la Marne.

Family in Tain-l'Hermitage since 1808; Polydor Chapoutier bought first vines 1879. Largest Hermitage vineyard owner; biodynamic; produces across Cote-Rotie, Hermitage, Crozes, St-Joseph, Cornas, Chateauneuf.
Founded 1979 by Bruno Clair in Marsannay-la-Cote. 25 ha across 28 appellations in 7 villages spanning Marsannay, Fixin, Savigny-les-Beaune (La Dominode), Morey-Saint-Denis and Gevrey-Chambertin.
Founded 1999 by oenologist Sylvain Pataille in Marsannay-la-Cote. ~15 ha certified organic, all within or adjacent to Marsannay; champion of Aligote and precise Pinot Noir reds.
Feraud family in Chateauneuf-du-Pape with roots to 17th century; Pegau label since 1987. Traditional Grenache-led Chateauneuf, 22 ha; Cuvee da Capo is its rare flagship.
Founded 1837 by Henri-Marc de Venoge, based on Avenue de Champagne in Epernay. Owned by Lanson-BCC; ~700,000 bottles annually; first house to use illustrated labels.
Family estate in Saint-Cibard since 1610 on the plateau shared with Saint-Emilion and Pomerol. 51 ha biodynamic; historically Francs Cotes de Bordeaux, switched to Vin de France in 2017.
Founded 1864 by Eloy Lecanda in Valbuena de Duero, Valladolid. Owned by Alvarez family since 1982; 210 ha of vines on 985 ha estate; flagship Unico Tempranillo-led blend.

First Growth (promoted 1973) in Pauillac; ~90 ha of gravel-clay terroir, predominantly Cabernet Sauvignon. Pioneered estate bottling 1924 and the artist-label series under Baron Philippe de Rothschild.
Founded 1984 by Gregorio Garcia Alvarez in Gumiel de Mercado. 140 ha of high-altitude Tempranillo (800-900 m) plus 12 ha of Albillo Mayor; non-irrigated, traditional viticulture.
Founded by Davy Dosnon in Avirey-Lingey, Cote des Bar (Aube), first vintage 2007. ~5 ha of Pinot Noir-led vines on Kimmeridgian limestone; ferments in used Puligny barrels; converting to biodynamic.
Founded 1935; family-owned in Colchagua Valley. House specialty Malbec with three Colchagua estates; flagship Viu 1 is Chile's only Malbec icon wine; ranks on World's Best Vineyards list.
Founded 1902 by Italian immigrant Nicola Catena. Pyramid-shaped winery in Agrelo, Lujan de Cuyo; pioneered high-altitude Malbec with parcels in Adrianna (Gualtallary), Nicasia, Domingo and Angelica.
Grower-producer in Meurville (Aube). Remi Leroy returned home in 2006 with a Bordeaux oenology degree; ~8 ha Pinot Noir-dominant on Jurassic clay-limestone, organic farming, ~60,000 bottles/year.
Cedric Bouchard's domaine in Celles-sur-Ource (Aube), founded 2000 with 1.09 ha. Single-parcel, single-variety, single-vintage, zero-dosage Champagnes; organic farming on Kimmeridgian limestone.
Port house formed 1877 (Silva & Cosens, est. 1798); owned by Symington family since 1961. Flagship vineyards Quinta do Bomfim (Pinhao) and Quinta da Senhora da Ribeira; drier vintage Port style.

Fifth-generation family winery founded 1886 by Dr William Angove in Tea Tree Gully. Cellar doors in McLaren Vale (Warboys Vineyard) and Renmark; Australia's largest organic grape grower (Nanya 260 ha).

Founded 1983 by Christian Moueix on the historic Napanook vineyard in Yountville, Napa Valley. 108-acre Bordeaux-style estate (Cab Sauv, Petit Verdot, Cab Franc); winery designed by Herzog & de Meuron.

Familia Torres founded 1870 in Vilafranca del Penedes; largest winery in Spain. Vineyards across Penedes, Conca de Barbera, Priorat, Costers del Segre and beyond; pioneer of organic and regenerative viticulture.
Founded 1995 as joint venture between Eduardo Chadwick (Errazuriz) and Robert Mondavi; Chadwick sole owner since 2004. 42 ha biodynamic hillside vineyard, Bordeaux-blend icon wine.
Family-owned in Fumane di Valpolicella since 16th century, modern estate built by Giovanni Allegrini from the 1960s. 90 ha in Sant'Ambrogio and Fumane; renowned for Amarone Classico and La Poja.
Cava house in Sant Sadurni d'Anoia, Penedes; roots to 1861 (Casa Sala). World's largest Cava producer; classic Macabeo/Xarel-lo/Parellada blend by traditional method.
Founded 1999 by Michael Twelftree and Richard Mintz in Marananga, Barossa. Single-vineyard Shiraz focus across Barossa, McLaren Vale, Clare Valley, Eden Valley, Adelaide Hills and Heathcote.
Founded 2001 by Marilisa and Walter Allegrini (of Veneto's Allegrini) in Bolgheri DOC. 70 ha across four vineyards; Bordeaux-blend Sondraia and 100% Cabernet Franc 'Dedicato a Walter'.

Founded 1946 by Jean Orens Ferraton in Tain-l'Hermitage. Biodynamic since 1998; estate vines in Hermitage (Le Meal), plus negociant cuvees from Crozes, St-Joseph, Cote-Rotie, Condrieu, Cornas, Chateauneuf.

Founded 1883 by Don Melchor Concha y Toro at Pirque, Maipo Valley with imported Bordeaux varieties. Largest Latin American wine producer; flagship Don Melchor from Puente Alto; vineyards across most Chilean valleys.

Founded 1859 in Vertus, Cote des Blancs. ~200 ha of Chardonnay (Cote des Blancs Grand/Premier Cru) and Pinot Noir (Montagne de Reims); led by Carol Duval-Leroy since 1991; biggest house in Cote des Blancs.

Established 1897, restored 19th-century gravity-flow winery in Russell, Maipu. Estate vineyards span Maipu and Uco Valley (Gualtallary, La Arboleda in Tupungato); Wine Enthusiast 2023 New World Winery of the Year.

Founded 1858 in Vosne-Romanee; renamed Arnoux-Lachaux 2008. ~14 ha across Vosne-Romanee, Nuits-St-Georges, Chambolle-Musigny, Echezeaux, Romanee-Saint-Vivant; Charles Lachaux leads since 2015 with biodynamic shift.
